title: Simple CUDA Program Example: GPU Programming Fundamentals with Parallel Computing
description: Learn CUDA programming fundamentals with a complete example. Master GPU memory allocation, data transfer, kernel launching, and parallel computing concepts for high-performance applications using NVIDIA CUDA.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Standalone Spark Application in Scala: Twitter Streaming Example
description: Build a standalone Spark Streaming application in Scala that surfaces popular hashtags from the Twitter firehose, packaged with sbt and developed in the Eclipse IDE.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: AM Wave Generation and Plotting with Matplotlib Python: A Detailed Guide
description: Learn how to generate and plot Amplitude Modulation (AM) waves using the Matplotlib Python library. This tutorial demonstrates how to use Matplotlib to create AM waves with a specified carrier frequency and message frequency, and customize the appearance of the plot. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced developer, this guide will help you get started with AM generation using Matplotlib in Linux. Explore a range of examples and discover the potential of Matplotlib for data visualization and signal processing in Linux.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Creating Uber JARs for Spark Projects with sbt-assembly
description: Package a standalone Spark application into a single fat JAR using the sbt-assembly plugin. Covers plugin setup, merge strategies, and deploying the result.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Building a Simple Game with HTML5 Canvas and JavaScript
description: Build a simple bird-shooting game on an HTML5 canvas with plain JavaScript. Covers drawing, animation timing, keyboard events, and collision detection.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Measuring an RC Time Constant with an ATmega8
description: Measure an RC time constant in hardware using the ATmega8's ADC and timer. Covers the circuit, the C code, and a couple of test runs with different R-C values.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Install Apache Spark 2 on Ubuntu 16.04 and macOS
description: Install Apache Spark 2.0 on Ubuntu 16.04 and macOS. Covers Java setup, the Maven build, Hadoop integration, and environment configuration.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Install Apache Spark 3.5 on Linux (Ubuntu, CentOS)
description: Install Apache Spark 3.5 on Linux (Ubuntu, CentOS), binary distribution and from source. Covers Java/Python prerequisites, environment variables, standalone cluster, YARN and Kubernetes deploy modes, and HDFS integration.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Install Apache Spark on Ubuntu 14.04
description: Install Apache Spark 1.1.0 on Ubuntu 14.04. Covers Java, Scala, and git prerequisites, the sbt assembly build, the Spark shell, streaming MQTT, and rebuilding against a specific Hadoop version.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Introduction to AVR Microcontroller Programming
description: Master AVR microcontroller programming from scratch. Learn development environment setup, AVR-GCC compilation, programming with USBASP, and create your first LED blinking program with detailed code explanations and hardware concepts.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Introduction to Parallel Programing
description: This blog post provides an introduction to parallel programming on the GPU. It explores the concept of parallel computing, where large problems are broken down into smaller pieces and executed simultaneously. The post discusses the technical trends driving parallel computing on the GPU, such as the increasing number of transistors on processors and the need for power efficiency. It also introduces CUDA (Computer Unified Device Architecture), a parallel programming platform developed by NVIDIA. The CUDA programming model allows developers to leverage the power of both the CPU and GPU in their programs. This blog post serves as a summary of the author's notes from the Udacity course 'Intro to Parallel Programming'.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: LCD Interfacing with an MSP430 Microcontroller: Displaying ADC Readings
description: Wire a 16x2 LCD to an MSP430 LaunchPad and display live voltage readings from the onboard ADC. Covers the 4-bit interface, ADC configuration, and the C code.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: MQTT Publisher and Subscriber in Scala with Eclipse Paho
description: A minimal MQTT publisher and subscriber in Scala using Eclipse Paho, plus how to run a local Mosquitto broker to test them against.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: A Web Paint App with Flask and MongoDB
description: Build a small web paint app backed by Flask and MongoDB. Covers the HTML5 canvas front-end, the Flask routes that accept drawing data, and the MongoDB schema.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: HTML5 Canvas Paint Application with JavaScript
description: A small browser-based paint app built on an HTML5 canvas. Drawing tools, colors, and mouse-event handling, plus notes on deploying with Google App Engine and Flask.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Apache Spark Performance Tuning: Spill, Skew, Shuffle, Storage, and Serialization
description: A practical guide to tuning Apache Spark jobs. Covers the five areas that matter most in production: spill, skew, shuffle, storage, and serialization. Includes runnable PySpark examples.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: How to Run a PySpark Notebook with Docker
description: Run PySpark in a Jupyter notebook via Docker. Walks through the official jupyter/pyspark-notebook image, a docker-compose setup for persistent work, and how to connect from your browser.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Running Arduino Code on a Standalone ATmega8
description: Run Arduino sketches on a bare ATmega8 using the Arduino IDE, a USB ISP, and the right fuse-byte settings for an 8 MHz clock.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Running Mesos 0.13 on Ubuntu 12.04
description: Install Apache Mesos 0.13 on Ubuntu 12.04 and start a single-master cluster. Covers apt packages, building from source, launching master and slave, and configuring a client machine.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Building Self-Contained PySpark Applications
description: Go from the `pyspark` REPL to a self-contained Python application you can ship with `spark-submit`. Covers project layout, dependencies, and submission.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: How to Build a USBtinyISP: Low-Cost DIY AVR Programmer
description: Build a low-cost DIY USB programmer for AVR microcontrollers. Walks through the circuit, fuse-bit configuration with avrdude, and how to program ATtiny and ATmega chips.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Advanced AM Modulation Analysis with Matplotlib
description: Go beyond basic AM waveforms. Build a reusable Matplotlib + NumPy analyzer that calculates modulation index, inspects sidebands via FFT, measures signal quality, and handles noise.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Advanced PySpark Performance Optimization Techniques
description: Advanced PySpark tuning beyond the basics. Covers adaptive query execution, dynamic partition pruning, predicate pushdown, cost-based optimization, and memory management patterns.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: Advanced PySpark Design Patterns: Implementation Examples
description: Four advanced design patterns applied to PySpark pipelines, with runnable examples: strategy, decorator, command, and template method.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: ATtiny2313 USB Programming Guide
description: Go deeper on the ATtiny2313 USB programmer: fuse strategies, component selection, PCB considerations, recovery from bricked chips, and debugging tools.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: PySpark Design Patterns Tutorial: Factory, Singleton, Builder, Observer, and Pipeline Patterns with Examples
description: A hands-on PySpark tutorial showing how to apply five classic software design patterns, factory, singleton, builder, observer, and pipeline, to build cleaner, more maintainable data pipelines. Full code examples you can run.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra

title: PySpark Design Patterns Quick Reference
description: One-page cheat sheet with runnable snippets for the five core PySpark design patterns: factory, singleton, builder, observer, and pipeline.
author: Prabeesh Keezhathra
